title: Operator TUI: Carbonyl OIDC browser session modes
summary: Add two explicit OIDC-capable Carbonyl browser modes for the Operator TUI center viewport: (A) Carbonyl as the real browser session where provider cookies/session storage stay inside the Carbonyl profile, and (B) Carbonyl as login UI only while Ananta owns the OAuth/OIDC callback and token exchange. Both modes must support Keycloak first and Google as an explicit provider compatibility test.
important_design_decision: Carbonyl is allowed to render and hold browser cookies for the active browser profile, but OAuth tokens must be owned by Ananta AuthController by default. A provider-full-browser-session mode may exist, but it must be explicitly enabled and isolated per session/profile.

keycloak.reason: Ananta controls keycloak.ananta.de and can configure redirect URIs, client type, PKCE and test realms.

google.reason: Google OAuth flows may reject non-standard or embedded user agents. Carbonyl should be tested, not assumed.

modes.description: Carbonyl acts like the user's browser for the web app. Provider cookies, local/session storage and authenticated browser state remain in Carbonyl's isolated user-data-dir for that Ananta session.
flow: Operator TUI opens Carbonyl in CenterViewport
Carbonyl navigates to local Ananta web app or external provider login page
User logs in via Keycloak/Google in Carbonyl
Redirect returns to the web app or loopback callback
The logged-in page remains usable inside the same Carbonyl browser profile
benefits: Closest to a real browser experience inside the TUI
Useful for testing web apps that rely on browser cookies/session storage
OIDC session is visible and debuggable in the center viewport
risks: Provider may reject Carbonyl as unsupported/disallowed user agent
Browser profile can contain sensitive cookies
Remote pages can execute JavaScript inside the Carbonyl browser context
required_controls: clear warning when Google/provider rejects the user agent

modes.description: Carbonyl is only the login/consent UI. Ananta starts a local loopback callback listener, validates state/nonce, exchanges the authorization code with PKCE, and owns tokens outside the browser document.
flow: AuthController creates state, nonce, PKCE verifier/challenge and loopback callback
AuthController validates state/nonce and exchanges code for tokens
Ananta creates its own local session for the TUI/web app
Carbonyl loads the local authenticated Ananta page without receiving raw provider tokens
benefits: Works cleanly with native-app style OAuth/OIDC
Can fall back to external system browser if Carbonyl is rejected
risks: Requires robust local callback listener lifecycle
Needs careful token storage and refresh policy
required_controls: tokens never embedded in generated HTML
structured audit events without token values

implementation_tasks.title: Add AuthController contract for Carbonyl-initiated OIDC login
details: Create an AuthController that can start OIDC authorization_code_pkce flows for configured providers.
Generate and store state, nonce, PKCE verifier/challenge per login attempt.
Expose create_authorization_request(provider_id, mode) and complete_callback(callback_url).
Never expose access_token, refresh_token or id_token to generated browser HTML unless explicitly configured for a dedicated unsafe test.
acceptance_criteria: AuthController generates unique state/nonce/verifier per login attempt.
Callback with wrong state is rejected.
Callback with missing/invalid code is rejected with a useful error.
Unit tests verify token values are redacted from logs and status messages.

implementation_tasks.title: Implement local loopback callback listener
details: Bind to 127.0.0.1 on a random free port by default.
Accept exactly one callback per login attempt unless configured otherwise.
Render a minimal local success/failure page for Carbonyl after callback.
Stop automatically after success, failure or timeout.
Reject non-loopback listener configuration unless explicitly allowed for development.
acceptance_criteria: Listener starts and returns a concrete redirect_uri.
Listener shuts down after first valid callback.
Timeout releases the port and reports degraded login state.
Non-loopback bind is blocked by default.

implementation_tasks.title: Add isolated Carbonyl browser profiles
details: Create per-session user-data-dir paths under configured profile_root.
Default to ephemeral profiles cleaned up on exit.
Support named persisted profiles only when explicitly enabled.
Associate provider cookies/session storage with the selected profile.
Add a command to clear the current Carbonyl profile.
acceptance_criteria: Two login sessions use different profile directories by default.
Ephemeral profile is deleted after normal exit.
Persisted profile is never created unless persist_named_profiles=true.
Profile paths cannot escape the configured profile_root.

implementation_tasks.title: Wire Carbonyl real browser session mode
details: Add explicit command center.browser.oidc.open_provider_session.
Open the provider/app URL inside Carbonyl with the selected isolated profile.
Keep the browser session alive in the CenterViewport until the user exits.
Show status: provider, profile mode, network policy and login state if known.
Handle provider errors like unsupported/disallowed user agent without crashing the TUI.
acceptance_criteria: Keycloak login page can be opened in Carbonyl session mode.
After successful login, cookies remain in the same Carbonyl profile for that session.
Exiting browser mode preserves or destroys profile according to policy.
Google provider failure is shown as compatibility failure, not as Ananta crash.

implementation_tasks.title: Wire default Ananta-owned OIDC callback mode
details: Make ananta_owned_oidc_callback the default OIDC mode.
Start loopback listener before launching Carbonyl authorization URL.
After callback, exchange authorization code server-side/client-side according to provider client type.
Create an Ananta-local authenticated session and load a local success page in Carbonyl.
Do not place provider tokens in URL fragments, HTML, logs, TUI status lines or temp files.
acceptance_criteria: Keycloak PKCE login completes and creates an Ananta session.
Raw tokens are not present in generated HTML, temp files or normal logs.
Logout invalidates Ananta session and optionally clears Carbonyl profile.
Failure paths return the TUI to a usable state.

implementation_tasks.title: Add Keycloak and Google compatibility smoke tests
details: Smoke test Keycloak against keycloak.ananta.de when explicitly configured.
Smoke test Google only when a local client_id is provided and user explicitly opts in.
Capture provider outcome categories: success, provider_rejected_user_agent, network_error, callback_timeout, invalid_state, token_exchange_failed.
Document that Google compatibility must be tested regularly and may change.
acceptance_criteria: Default CI does not require external providers.
Smoke script can run in offline mock mode.
Smoke script reports provider compatibility as structured JSON.
Docs explain both OIDC modes and when to use each.

implementation_tasks.title: Add redacted audit events for OIDC browser sessions
details: Emit structured events for login_start, callback_received, state_validated, token_exchange_success, token_exchange_failed, provider_rejected, logout, profile_cleanup.
Redact tokens, auth codes and cookies.
Include provider_id, mode, profile_mode and error category only.
acceptance_criteria: Audit logs are useful for debugging without containing secrets.
Tests fail if token-like values appear in audit output.
User-visible errors are short and actionable.

mermaid_architecture:
flowchart TD
    A[Operator TUI command] --> B{OIDC mode}
    B -->|real browser session| C[Carbonyl with isolated profile]
    C --> D[Provider login page]
    D --> E[Provider cookies/session stay in Carbonyl profile]
    B -->|Ananta-owned callback| F[AuthController creates state nonce PKCE]
    F --> G[Loopback callback 127.0.0.1 random port]
    F --> H[Carbonyl opens authorization URL]
    H --> I[Provider redirects to loopback]
    I --> J[Validate state nonce and exchange code]
    J --> K[Ananta session store owns tokens]
    K --> L[Carbonyl loads local authenticated Ananta page]
acceptance_summary: Both OIDC modes exist and are explicit.
Ananta-owned callback mode is the secure default.
Carbonyl real browser session mode uses isolated profiles.
Keycloak is the first-class test provider.
Google is supported as compatibility probe, not assumed guaranteed.
TUI remains usable after provider failures, timeout or user cancel.
